title: OpenEye OS Install and Recovery Guide
description: USB recovery creation, reinstall flow, network restore, Apex update, and compatibility checks.

sections.title: Create USB Recovery Drive
sections.summary: Build the correct installer media before touching the server.
steps: In OWS, check Help > About and confirm image version.
Download matching ISO and launch Rufus.
Choose GPT + UEFI partition scheme.
Use FAT32 for Linux images and NTFS for Windows images.
Write USB, wait for verification, then safely eject.

notes.text: Wrong image version can cause failed boot or partial installs.

images.caption: Confirm image version in OWS before creating media.

sections.title: Recovery and Reinstall
sections.summary: Boot from USB and complete guided recovery.
steps: Insert recovery USB and reboot the recorder.
Open boot menu: F12 (most), F7 (MV), ESC (MD).
Select UEFI USB device from boot menu.
Run recovery wizard and enter serial when requested.
Allow reboot and finish first-boot setup checks.

notes.text: Capture network values before recovery so they can be restored quickly.

images.caption: Use password when BIOS prompt appears.

sections.title: Network, Apex, and Validation
sections.summary: Restore config and confirm operations after recovery.
steps: Re-apply network settings from pre-reset notes.
Open Apex settings and run Software Update.
Import site config backup when available.
Validate camera online state and recording health.

images.caption: Open software update in Apex settings.
Paste package URL and start update.
Check current OS and hardware info.

title: Claude and VS Code AI Dev Guide
description: Minimal setup for Claude API, local Node project, and GitHub publish flow.

sections.title: Environment Setup
sections.summary: Create project scaffold and load credentials securely.
steps: Create Anthropic account and generate API key.
Install VS Code and Node.js.
Create project folder and src directory.
Add .env with CLAUDE_API_KEY value.

commands.label: Scaffold
commands.value:
mkdir ai-project
cd ai-project
mkdir src

notes.text: Do not commit .env files containing API keys.

sections.title: Basic Script
sections.summary: Single file message call with dotenv config.

commands.label: src/index.js

sections.title: Publish to GitHub
sections.summary: Commit local project and push first main branch.
steps: Initialize git and create first commit.
Create remote repository on GitHub.
Set branch to main and push.

commands.label: Git Commands
commands.value:
git init
git add .
git commit -m "initial commit"
git branch -M main
git remote add origin https://github.com/YOUR_USERNAME/ai-project.git
git push -u origin main

title: Raspberry Pi OS Gaming Dev and Network Guide
description: RetroArch and RetroPie basics, VS Code setup, network tools, and quick maintenance commands.

sections.title: RetroArch and Core Setup
sections.summary: Install emulator stack and core packages.
steps: Install RetroArch from apt for a stable baseline.
Use Flatpak if you need newer upstream builds.
Install common libretro cores from apt.
Use RetroPie setup for N64 options when apt lacks packages.

commands.label: RetroArch
commands.value: sudo apt update && sudo apt install -y retroarch retroarch-assets

sections.title: Performance and Freeze Recovery
sections.summary: Tune performance and recover quickly from hangs.
steps: Use lr-snes9x2010 for balanced SNES speed/compatibility.
Lower internal resolution for N64-heavy titles.
Verify power supply and throttling state.
Kill stuck emulator processes from TTY if UI freezes.

commands.label: Force Close
commands.value:
killall -9 retroarch
killall -9 emulationstation
sudo reboot

notes.text: Use legal ROMs only and verify sources before installing packages.

sections.title: Developer and Network Toolkit
sections.summary: Install tools for diagnostics and routine maintenance.

commands.label: Network Tools
commands.value: sudo apt install -y nmap iftop tcpdump iperf3
